item.title: Anthropic to challenge DOD’s supply-chain label in court
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei said he plans to challenge the Department of Defense's designation of the AI firm as a supply-chain risk. He claims most Anthropic customers are unaffected by the label.

item.title: DiligenceSquared uses AI, voice agents to make M&A research affordable
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Instead of relying on expensive management consultants, the startup uses AI voice agents to conduct interviews with customers of the companies the PE firms are considering buying.

item.title: AWS launches a new AI agent platform specifically for healthcare
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: AWS is launching Amazon Connect Health, an AI agent platform that will help with patient scheduling, documentation, and patient verification.

item.title: US reportedly considering sweeping new chip export controls
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: In an alleged drafted proposal, the U.S. government would play a role in every chip export sale regardless of which country it's coming from.

item.title: It’s official: The Pentagon has labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The Department of Defense has officially labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk, making the AI firm the first American company with the label. Meanwhile, the DOD continues to use Anthropic's AI in Iran.

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Luma launches creative AI agents powered by its new ‘Unified Intelligence’ models
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Luma introduced Luma Agents, powered by its new “Unified Intelligence” models, designed to coordinate multiple AI systems and generate end-to-end creative work across text, images, video and audio.

item.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with Pro and Thinking versions
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: GPT-5.4 is billed as "our most capable and efficient frontier model for professional work."

item.title: Cursor is rolling out a new kind of agentic coding tool
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Called Automations, the new system gives users a way to automatically launch agents within their coding environment, triggered by a new addition to the codebase, a Slack message, or a simple timer.

item.title: Meta sued over AI smart glasses’ privacy concerns, after workers reviewed nudity, sex, and other footage
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Lawyers say Meta's marketing materials promised privacy and user control over sharing footage. But an investigation found that subcontractors are reviewing footage from customers' glasses.

item.title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei could still be trying to make a deal with Pentagon
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Anthropic's $200 million contract with the Department of Defense broke down due to disagreements over giving the military unrestricted access to its AI.

item.title: Netflix buys Ben Affleck’s AI filmmaking company InterPositive
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: InterPositive isn't trying to make AI actors or synthetic performances. Rather, the company has created a model that helps production teams work with footage from their own productions to help make edits in post-production.

item.title: How 1,000+ customer calls shaped a breakout enterprise AI startup
item.dc:creator: Maggie Nye, Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: On this episode of Build Mode, David Park joins Isabelle Johannessen to discuss how he and his team are intentionally iterating, fundraising, and scaling Narada.

item.title: Lio raises $30M from Andreessen Horowitz and others to automate enterprise procurement
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: AI procurement startup Lio announced a $30 million Series A in a round led by Andreessen Horowitz.

item.title: Jensen Huang says Nvidia is pulling back from OpenAI and Anthropic, but his explanation raises more questions than it answers
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said Wednesday that his company's investments in OpenAI and Anthropic will likely be its last — but his explanation may not tell the whole story.

item.title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei calls OpenAI’s messaging around military deal ‘straight up lies,’ report says
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Anthropic gave up its contract with the Pentagon over AI safety disagreements -- then, OpenAI swooped in.

item.title: Apple Music to add Transparency Tags to distinguish AI music, says report
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: The label or distributor has to opt in to tagging their music as AI, so it's unclear how effective this intervention will be.

item.title: Google Search rolls out Gemini’s Canvas in AI Mode to all US users
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Canvas in AI Mode is available to U.S. users in English for creating plans, projects, apps, and more.

item.title: Decagon completes first tender offer at $4.5B valuation
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The AI-powered customer support startup is the latest example of a fast-growing, young company that's providing employee liquidity.

item.title: The US military is still using Claude — but defense-tech clients are fleeing
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: As the U.S. continues its aerial attack on Iran, Anthropic models are being used for many targeting decisions.
